title: Hero trailer and first viewport
brief: Push the first viewport toward a premium movie teaser. Improve composition, typography, video treatment, copy tension, responsive framing, and the sense that the next section is just visible below the fold.
acceptance: Hero still says "He's back." or a stronger teaser variant with the same reveal energy.
Video remains the dominant first-viewport asset and does not look like a small preview card.
Text remains legible on mobile and desktop without overlapping controls or the video focal point.
No starter Next.js, Vercel, or generic template language is introduced.

title: OpenGL shader and GSAP motion pass
brief: Make the WebGL and GSAP layer feel cinematic without tanking performance. Add or refine shader atmosphere, scroll-linked movement, pointer reaction, reveal timing, and reduced-motion behavior.
acceptance: Shader canvas renders nonblank on desktop and mobile.
No animation causes layout shift, text overlap, or blocked interactions.
Client-side code cleans up event listeners, animation frames, GSAP contexts, and WebGL resources.

title: Origin story and character mythology
brief: Deepen Droyd, the mad scientist, and the noir mythology. Improve the story beats, section rhythm, evidence motifs, and emotional stakes while keeping copy sharp and trailer-like.
acceptance: Droyd and the mad scientist both feel specific, memorable, and connected.
Copy is cinematic and concise, not explanatory marketing filler.
Sections scan well on mobile and desktop.
The page still feels like an interactive film reveal, not documentation.

title: Responsive, accessibility, and production polish
brief: Raise the production quality bar: metadata, accessibility labels, color contrast, responsive constraints, documentation, and build hygiene.
acceptance: npm run typecheck passes from client.
npm run build passes from client.
No default create-next-app assets or copy are used by the app.
